{"step_by_step_thinking": "Retinitis pigmentosa is a condition that can lead to optic atrophy due to the degeneration of retinal ganglion cells. Central retinal arterial occlusion (CRAO) can cause optic atrophy as it leads to ischemia in the optic nerve. Methanol poisoning can also cause optic atrophy as methanol is toxic to the optic nerve. However, polypoidal choroidal vasculopathy is a condition that primarily affects the choroid and retina, and does not typically lead to optic atrophy.", "answer_choice": "D"}
